Title: Manipulation of β-carotene levels in tomato fruits results in increased ABA content and extended shelf life

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates fruit ripening and shelf life
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous; inhibitor-based pharmacology also discussed, and E8 promoter cited for RNAi lines in discussion
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pharmacological inhibition of NCED reduced ABA in LCYb fruits, increased ethylene, and reversed firmness/water-loss/shelf-life traits
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Abamine, an NCED inhibitor, lowered ABA in LCYb-overexpressing fruits to near-WT levels. 2. This treatment increased ethylene production and reverted firmness and water-loss phenotypes. 3. NCED-mediated ABA biosynthesis is therefore required for the LCYb-dependent shelf-life phenotype, although NCED itself was not the transgene manipulated here.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pharmacological inhibition with abamine; ABA quantification; ethylene and firmness/water-loss assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abamine treatment at mature green stage
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA reduction can accelerate ripening and reduce shelf life
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ABA biosynthesis control point relevant for shelf-life engineering, but likely pleiotropic if constitutively altered
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because NCED enzymatic action was directly interrogated through abamine in Results, though the paper does not focus on a specific NCED gene isoform.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch point; upstream of ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes both ends of lycopene to form β-carotene, increasing flux into cyclic carotenoids and downstream xanthophyll/ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported in tomato fruit as a key/rate-limiting control step for ABA biosynthesis during late ripening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: β-carotene is provitamin A; ABA regulates fruit ripening, firmness, water loss, cuticle deposition, and shelf life
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Tomato PDS promoter, fruit/chromoplast-associated inducible promoter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expression peaks at breaker and breaker+4 stages in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases β-carotene and ABA accumulation, decreases ethylene production, and extends tomato fruit shelf life with increased firmness and reduced water loss
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fruit-specific overexpression of Arabidopsis LCYb caused a 4- to 10-fold increase in β-carotene and other cyclic carotenes and reduced linear carotenes in ripe tomato fruit. 2. LCYb-overexpressing fruits showed strongly increased ABA and ABA catabolites, reduced ethylene production/ACO activity, delayed softening, thicker cuticle, altered cell wall properties, and extended shelf life. 3. Abamine treatment reduced ABA and reversed the shelf-life phenotype, supporting that LCYb acts through increased β-carotene-derived ABA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fruit-specific overexpression in tomato; carotenoid profiling; ABA quantification; ethylene and ACO assays; firmness/water loss assays; transcript profiling; pharmacological inhibition with abamine
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix EU-TOM3 microarray, GEO: GSE108415; metabolomics by LC-HRMS, LC-DAD-APCI/ESI-HRMS, GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-grown plants; fruit ripening stages MG, B, B+4, B+10, B+15; postharvest storage at room temperature; abamine treatment at MG stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced lycopene and nonpolar antioxidant activity; broad ripening-associated metabolic remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful metabolic engineering target to simultaneously enhance provitamin A and extend fruit shelf life; may be combined with ripening/cuticle traits, but reduced lycopene and altered ripening should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study argues that β-cyclization becomes decisive for ABA production in tomato fruit late ripening, unlike the canonical view emphasizing NCED as rate-limiting in leaves.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Control of fruit ripening and shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fruit ripening-associated ethylene biosynthesis/signaling and downstream ripening metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Ripening-associated genes including ethylene biosynthetic genes and other ripening genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Necessary positive regulator of ripening/ethylene-associated genes; transcript strongly repressed in LCYb-overexpressing fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression of RIN expression correlates with reduced ethylene and delayed ripening/extended shelf life in LCYb-overexpressing fruits
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ripening regulator tightly linked to ethylene output and late ripening state
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA-associated regulation/crosstalk; NAC-NOR acts upstream according to cited background
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ABA-ethylene-ripening regulatory network; proposed indirect negative association with elevated ABA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CNR (promoter binding dependency mentioned in discussion)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: During ripening; strongly repressed from breaker stage onward in LCYb-overexpressing fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; correlation network analysis; comparison in LCYb transgenic fruits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RIN transcript abundance was strongly reduced from breaker stage through ripening in LCYb-overexpressing fruits. 2. RIN was negatively co-regulated with ABA and positively associated with ethylene/ripening state. 3. Its altered expression supports involvement of ABA–ethylene crosstalk in the shelf-life phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix EU-TOM3 microarray, GEO: GSE108415; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stages; LCYb overexpression background
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression of ripening regulator may delay softening and alter normal ripening-associated metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker/regulatory node for ripening modulation, though broad pleiotropic effects make direct manipulation less precise than metabolic engineering of LCYb
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because its expression kinetics were directly tested in Results and integrated into the causal model.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Control of fruit ripening and shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ripening control linked to ethylene biosynthesis and ABA crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Ripening-associated genes; acts upstream of RIN according to background cited in paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Expression increased in LCYb-overexpressing fruits and positively co-regulated with ABA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction of NAC-NOR correlates with elevated ABA and altered ripening network in LCYb-overexpressing fruits
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ripening regulator centrally connected with ABA and ethylene networks
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ABA-ethylene-ripening network; positive association with ABA in correlation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Large increase in expression at breaker stage; upregulated in LCYb-overexpressing fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; correlation network analysis; comparison in LCYb transgenic fruits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC-NOR expression showed a large increase at breaker stage in LCYb-overexpressing fruits. 2. NAC-NOR was strongly positively co-regulated with ABA in network analysis. 3. The data support a model in which elevated β-carotene-derived ABA perturbs the ripening regulatory network involving NOR and ethylene.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix EU-TOM3 microarray, GEO: GSE108415; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stages; LCYb overexpression background
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation of major ripening regulator may broadly affect ripening and fruit quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important diagnostic regulator for ABA/ethylene-ripening crosstalk, though not the primary engineering target in this study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because its expression was directly assayed and used in the mechanistic model.
